target: ISS-2307 OBE: canary-motive-telegraphing wording already corrected by EXP-513

obe_resolutions.reason: Hole 2 recommendation (strip recipient-naming from both patches) was satisfied as an incidental side-effect of EXP-513 (baby-batch-5, commit 819cb05, 2026-05-31T08:04Z). EXP-513 explicitly targeted ISS-2306/2308/2309/2310 (parallel-structure, single-sentence, unilaterality, we-voice) and rewrote both passages to text that ALSO removes the recipient-naming flagged by ISS-2307.

patterns_checked: "we want it visible at the nav level"
verification.current_reading_guide_clause: surfaced as its own tab because it is a narrowly-scoped methodology critique with a structurally simple fix — kept separate to make the recommendation easy to find
verification.current_tab_top_clause: it is a narrowly-scoped methodology error with a structurally simple fix — separating the prediction document from the observation document and timestamping each independently
verification.rationale: Both clauses now describe the critique without naming the recipient (no "dome author" reference) and without first-person editorial "we want" framing. The strategic gift-framing that hole 2 specifically diagnosed ("this critique was strategically elevated because we want the dome author to see and fix it") is no longer textually visible. The substantive content critique (narrow scope, structural fix) is retained.
verification.built_html_check: docs/index.html post-rebuild contains both new clauses verbatim and zero occurrences of the three recipient-naming patterns above.

kernel_of_truth: The curmudgeon was correct that the original patches telegraphed the canary motive. Hole 2 was a real problem — the original wording named the recipient ("the dome author") and the strategic intent ("we want it visible") in the same sentence, which a sufficiently attentive reader could deduce as bait. EXP-513 fixed this completely as a side effect of fixing the parallel-structure tone problems. The fix happens to be the same shape hole 2 recommended (strip recipient-naming) even though EXP-513 was scoped to a different set of holes.

anticipated_objections.objection: EXP-513 was scoped to ISS-2306/2308/2309/2310 — closing ISS-2307 against it is post-hoc credit-claiming.
anticipated_objections.response: Pattern-level verification confirms the textual fix: zero occurrences of the three recipient-naming patterns the curmudgeon hole 2 recommendation flagged for removal, in both build-scripts/generate-html.js (the source) and docs/index.html (the built output). The closure is on the basis of observable text-state, not crediting EXP-513 retroactively. If the operator wants the audit trail to attribute the fix to EXP-513 explicitly, that is fine; if the operator prefers to leave EXP-513 attribution-as-is and just record ISS-2307 as resolved by absorption, the substance is the same: the textual concern is gone from HEAD.

anticipated_objections.objection: Sibling ISS-2311 (EXP-488 in tracker, hole 6: visible-rationale strategy is circular) is NOT closed by EXP-513 — why is one closed but not the other?
anticipated_objections.response: Hole 6 recommended an annotation on the ISS-2218 closure record acknowledging that the visible-rationale strategy makes the placement defensible-not-coherent. That is a separate piece of substantive work (annotating an existing closed-issue record), not a text fix to the rationale paragraphs. EXP-513 only modified the rationale paragraph text; it did not touch ISS-2218. ISS-2311 still needs its own work and remains correctly assigned to analyst.

anticipated_objections.objection: Should this OBE be processed by analyst-baby (verification class) rather than Opus analyst?
anticipated_objections.response: Yes in principle — ISS-2307 is a verification-class item under the PROP-034 boundary (status==assigned-analyst, severity==moderate, work consists of textual verification + ISS closure). It surfaced to Opus this run because the decider routed it as deep-attack via class_hint, and the tracker entry was authored without setting assigned_to=analyst-baby. The correct future remediation is for decider to route similar canary-tone holes through baby. For this single instance, processing as OBE here costs less than re-routing through baby (one-EXP authoring overhead is paid either way; consolidating the OBE-close in the same run that diagnosed it keeps the audit trail single-stop).

summary_for_decider: ISS-2307 (Canary motive partially telegraphed) is OBE. The two recipient-naming phrases the curmudgeon hole 2 recommendation flagged for removal are no longer in build-scripts/generate-html.js or docs/index.html as of commit 819cb05 (EXP-513, 2026-05-31T08:04Z). EXP-513 explicitly targeted four different ISSs (2306/2308/2309/2310) but its replacement wording also eliminated the recipient-naming hole 2 was about. Verification: grep -c "we can hand the dome author|we want it visible|the dome author can fix" returns 0 in both source and built HTML; current text "a narrowly-scoped methodology critique with a structurally simple fix — kept separate to make the recommendation easy to find" satisfies hole 2 recommendation Option B-style (recipient-name stripped, third-person, no editorial "we"). Recommended action: close ISS-2307 (resolution: "OBE-resolved-by-EXP-513"), flip EXP-484 tracker entry to status=complete with output_file=EXP-517, no further patches needed, no build run needed (HEAD already has the fix). Sibling ISS-2311 (EXP-488) is NOT closed by this — it needs separate work annotating the ISS-2218 closure record.
